report_name: EU's NIS2 Directive Imposes Strict Cyber Rules, Personal Liability for Logistics Sector Management

identities.name: European Union
identities.description: The governing body responsible for creating and implementing the NIS2 Directive (Directive (EU) 2022/2555) to strengthen cybersecurity across critical sectors.

identities.name: Federal Office for Information Security (BSI)
identities.description: Germany's competent national authority mentioned as an example of an organization that will receive 'early warning' incident reports under the NIS2 Directive.

identities.name: Transport and Logistics Sector
identities.description: A sector classified as 'essential' under the NIS2 Directive, subject to the strictest rules. This includes companies involved in road, rail, air, and water transport, postal and courier services, and warehouse and storage providers.
